description: Civilian Everyday Life Scenario Library — scenarios 41 through 60. Covers family dynamics, parenting, caregiving, and relationship stress. Each scenario includes activating event, likely emotions, Four Blocks methodology fix, and rational redirection protocol for RAG ingestion.
source: NotebookLM cross-reference against My4Blocks methodology backbone

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling frustrated when a child repeatedly ignores instructions or behaves poorly.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Anger Formula (A = ET + S) and Seven Irrational Beliefs. The parent is demanding the child behave in accordance with their standard — a should belief about a developing, fallible young person.
scenarios.rational_redirection: Children are Fallible Human Beings in development. I would prefer they followed instructions, but demanding it only disturbs me. I address the behavior calmly and accept that children act exactly as they should given their current stage of development.

scenarios.activating_event: Worrying about a child's safety when they are away from home.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Anxiety Formula (AX = WI + AW + ICSI) — What If thinking. The parent is projecting danger onto a future that has not occurred and treating the possibility as a certainty.
scenarios.rational_redirection: My child is not in danger right now — that is a story in my head. I cannot control every condition of their world. Staying Now. Use the Centering Breath and let go of the What If.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling guilty after losing patience with a family member.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Guilt Formula (G = W1 + W2). The user is rating their loss of patience as Wrongness and attaching Worthlessness to themselves as a parent or family member.
scenarios.rational_redirection: My reaction was unhelpful — Behavior equals Behavior. I am not a bad person for losing patience. I am a Fallible Human Being. I apologize where needed, learn, and refuse the story that one reaction defines my worth.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling overwhelmed while caring for an aging parent or seriously ill relative.
scenarios.methodology_fix: SOS Method and Depression Formula (D = H1 + H2 + N). The user is overwhelmed by the totality of caregiving and generating helplessness and hopelessness about the situation.
scenarios.rational_redirection: I am standing this right now — which proves I can stand it. Use the SOS Method to identify what I am telling myself about the situation. Focus on just this task in this moment. I do not need to solve everything at once.

scenarios.activating_event: Replaying an argument with a spouse or partner long after it has ended.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Mental Contamination. The Narrator is replaying the argument and generating ongoing disturbance from an event that is already in the past.
scenarios.rational_redirection: Stop. This is the Narrator running the movie again. The argument is over. I am 100% responsible for continuing to replay it. Use Cognitive Stoppage and return to the Observer.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling anxious about an upcoming medical appointment or potential diagnosis.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Anxiety Formula and Mental Contamination — What If and Awfulizing. The Narrator is building a worst-case medical story before any facts are known.
scenarios.rational_redirection: The diagnosis does not exist yet. The horror story in my head is not reality. Staying Now. I will handle the facts when they arrive. Use the Centering Breath and return to this moment.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling discouraged when efforts to improve a relationship do not seem to be working.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Depression Formula (D = H1 + H2 + N) — Hopelessness and Helplessness. The user is treating slow relationship progress as evidence that improvement is impossible.
scenarios.rational_redirection: My efforts have not produced the result I wanted — that is a fact. My belief that it will never change is just a story. Hard is not the same as impossible. I continue to act effectively and accept the other person's fallibility.

scenarios.activating_event: Becoming irritated when household responsibilities are not shared fairly.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Anger Formula (A = ET + S) — Egocentric Thinking and Entitlement. The user is demanding that household responsibilities be distributed according to their personal standard of fairness.
scenarios.rational_redirection: I would prefer the responsibilities were shared more evenly, but demanding it only disturbs me. I address the practical problem directly and accept that my standard of fairness is not a universal law.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling guilty about not spending enough time with loved ones due to work demands.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Guilt Formula (G = W1 + W2) and BS Equation. The user is rating themselves as a bad family member based on time allocation rather than rating the specific behavior.
scenarios.rational_redirection: I want to spend more time with them, but not doing so does not make me worthless. Behavior equals Behavior. I adjust what I can and practice Unconditional Self-Acceptance.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling anxious about a child's future success and overall well-being.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Anxiety Formula — What If thinking and Awfulizing. The parent is projecting future failure or harm onto a child's life that has not yet unfolded.
scenarios.rational_redirection: My child's future is not determined yet. The story I am telling is not reality. Staying Now. I focus on being present and supportive today rather than being an emotional prisoner of a future that has not happened.

scenarios.activating_event: Becoming frustrated when a partner does not understand or acknowledge feelings.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Anger Formula (A = ET + S) and Need for Approval. The user is demanding emotional acknowledgment from their partner — treating understanding as a necessity rather than a preference.
scenarios.rational_redirection: I would prefer my partner understood me, but demanding it only disturbs me. They are a Fallible Human Being with their own limitations. I communicate clearly and accept their fallibility.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling emotionally drained from consistently supporting others while neglecting personal needs.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Depression Formula and Three Insights — Insight 1. The user is 100% responsible for the pattern of neglecting their own needs. Helplessness is being generated about a pattern that is within their power to change.
scenarios.rational_redirection: I am tired because I have given without replenishing. This is a fact, not a permanent state. I accept 100% responsibility for adjusting how I give. I cannot pour from an empty vessel.

scenarios.activating_event: Worrying about making the wrong parenting decision.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Anxiety Formula — Shoulds/Musts/Demands and Fear of Uncertainty. The parent is demanding perfect parenting decisions — an impossible standard applied to an inherently uncertain process.
scenarios.rational_redirection: There is no perfect parenting decision. I am a Fallible Human Being making the best choice I can with the information I have. Uncertainty is a fact of parenting, not a threat.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling regret after saying something hurtful to someone close.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Guilt Formula (G = W1 + W2). The user is rating hurtful words as Wrongness and attaching Worthlessness to themselves as a person for having said them.
scenarios.rational_redirection: My words were hurtful — Behavior equals Behavior. I am not a bad person for a moment of human fallibility. I apologize sincerely, repair what I can, and refuse the story that one moment defines my worth.

scenarios.activating_event: Becoming angry when family plans are disrupted or ignored by others.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Anger Formula (A = ET + S) — Egocentric Thinking and Should belief. The user is demanding that family members honor plans according to their personal standard.
scenarios.rational_redirection: I would have preferred the plans were honored, but demanding it only disturbs me. Family members are Fallible Human Beings. I address the disruption directly and accept the fallibility.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling sad when important relationships seem distant or emotionally disconnected.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Depression Formula (D = H1 + H2 + N) — Hopelessness and Namelessness. The user is treating the current disconnection as a permanent state and generating a story about the relationship being lost.
scenarios.rational_redirection: Distance in relationships is a current state, not a permanent verdict. I accept the disconnection as it is right now without the story that it will last forever. I take one small step toward connection today.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling anxious about balancing family responsibilities with work demands simultaneously.
scenarios.methodology_fix: SOS Method and Anxiety Formula — I Can't Stand It (ICSI). The user is treating the totality of simultaneous demands as unendurable rather than focusing on the single task in front of them.
scenarios.rational_redirection: I am standing this right now — that is proof I can stand it. Use the SOS Method. Focus on just this one responsibility in this one moment. I am not required to solve everything simultaneously.

scenarios.activating_event: Thinking repeatedly about a mistake made in caring for a loved one.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Mental Contamination and Guilt Formula. The Narrator is replaying the caregiving mistake and the user is applying the BS Equation — treating the mistake as evidence of their inadequacy as a caregiver.
scenarios.rational_redirection: Stop. This is the Narrator. The mistake happened because the conditions for it were met. I am a Fallible Human Being doing my best. Behavior equals Behavior. I learn from it and return to the Observer.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling frustrated when others do not appreciate the personal sacrifices made for the family.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Anger Formula (A = ET + S) — Seven Irrational Beliefs and Entitlement. The user is demanding recognition and gratitude for sacrifices — a should belief about how others must respond to their efforts.
scenarios.rational_redirection: I would prefer my sacrifices were acknowledged, but demanding gratitude only disturbs me. Others act according to their own awareness and limitations. I choose to give from my values, not from the expectation of return.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling overwhelmed by the emotional weight of consistently supporting others through their problems.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Depression Formula and SOS Method — Other Pity. The user is absorbing others' disturbance as their own emotional burden and generating helplessness about the accumulated weight.
scenarios.rational_redirection: I can have compassion for others' struggles without carrying them as my own. Use the SOS Method. I am most helpful when I am grounded. I am responsible for my emotional state, not for solving everyone else's problems.

implementation_notes.library_context: Civilian Scenario Library — family, parenting, and caregiving scenarios. This batch covers the most emotionally complex civilian contexts.
implementation_notes.source_tool: NotebookLM — raw scenario structure provided, methodology fix and rational redirection built by Claude against Four Blocks methodology
implementation_notes.enhanced_fields: Includes methodology_fix and rational_redirection fields consistent with full library format
implementation_notes.routing_note: Match scenario context to user input → identify stage → apply methodology fix → deliver rational redirection through civilian personality layer

stage_routing.pre_event: Route to anticipatory anxiety or pre-event behavior responses
stage_routing.during_event: Route to active coping — SOS method, centering breath, just this task
stage_routing.post_event: Route to processing responses — mental contamination protocol, formula application, rational redirection
